title: linaria amethystea var. subalpina
intro: Linaria amethystea var. subalpina is a rare, alpine species of the toadflax plant, characterized by its delicate, purple-blue flowers and slender stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Linaria amethystea var. subalpina are thought to include reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and potential relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's roots and stems in tincture form.
